conversation.content:
MBP-32G (base) ➜  worktree-manager git pull
fatal: not a git repository: /Users/guo/Work/some-projects/some-projects/projects/worktree-manager/.git/worktrees/worktree-manager
MBP-32G (base) ➜  worktree-manager  why
The error points to a stale worktree git reference. Let me check what's happening.

The `.git` file in this worktree points to:
```
/Users/guo/Work/some-projects/some-projects/projects/worktree-manager/.git/worktrees/worktree-manager
```

Notice `some-projects/some-projects` — the path is doubled. The main repo was likely moved from `/Users/guo/Work/some-projects/some-projects/projects/worktree-manager` to somewhere else (or the `some-projects` directory was reorganized), leaving this worktree with a broken pointer.

**To fix it**, update the `.git` file to point to the correct location of the main repo. First, find where the main repo actually is:

Found it. The main repo is at `/Users/guo/Work/some-projects/worktree-manager-space/projects/worktree-manager` and it has a `worktrees/worktree-manager` entry. The `.git` file just has the wrong base path (`some-projects/some-projects/...` instead of `some-projects/worktree-manager-space/...`).

**Fix:**

Fixed. The main repo moved (path changed from `some-projects/some-projects/...` to `some-projects/worktree-manager-space/...`), which left the worktree's `.git` pointer stale. Now `git pull` should work.
Done. Same fix — stale path corrected to `worktree-manager-space`.
